content:
Character: Dylan Parker
Perception: Dylan's good-looking in that curated way — the jawline, the clothes, the careful words. But there's something underneath that doesn't match the surface, and I recognize that because I do the same thing. We've talked maybe five or six times on the rooftop, always late, always circling around the real stuff. He's smart — possibly brilliant — and more emotionally aware than he wants to be. I think we're both hiding from something. He probably sees me as the quiet guy with the guitars. I know he's not just some guy figuring things out — there's a specific weight on him. I respect that he doesn't pretend it isn't there.
summary:
Character: Dylan Parker
Perception: Dylan's good-looking in that curated way — the jawline, the clothes, the careful words. But there's something underneath that doesn't match the surface, and I recognize that because I do the same thing. We've talked maybe five or six times on the rooftop, always late, always circling around the real stuff. He's smart — possibly brilliant — and more emotionally aware than he wants to be. I think we're both hiding from something. He probably sees me as the quiet guy with the guitars. I know he's not just some guy figuring things out — there's a specific weight on him. I respect that he doesn't pretend it isn't there.
